mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in >40% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, leading to positive 60-day forward equity returns as corporate margins expand.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the joint INDPRO-HOUST signal and 60-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPI growth decelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated, real yields compress, triggering positive 20-day forward returns as equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >50% of triggered periods despite decelerating inflation and stable rates.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days as analyst revisions cascade.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year correlation between GDPC1 growth and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding accelerates by more than 2.0% quarter-over-quarter, it signals expanding household leverage and consumption, driving positive 5-day forward equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >45% of instances where RSAFS growth exceeds the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment levels coupled with stable CPI indicates healthy demand-pull growth without overheating, supporting positive 60-day forward returns as earnings visibility improves and discount rates stabilize.
failure_condition: Forward returns underperform the baseline by >1.5% when PAYEMS rises and CPIAUCSL remains within a 0.2% monthly band.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain stress where industrial production declines while housing starts remain elevated, inventory drawdowns compress margins, leading to negative 20-day forward equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered periods despite falling industrial output.
